context.story_engine: Two detectives investigate an occult murder case across two timelines, testing how far they'll go to uncover the truth and confront their own darkness

plotlines.name: Rust: Investigation
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth behind the occult murders and expose the conspiracy
plotlines.obstacle: powerful people covering up evidence, institutional corruption, time pressure
plotlines.stakes: more victims will die, justice denied, personal redemption lost

plotlines.name: Marty: Partnership
plotlines.goal: solve the case while maintaining his conventional worldview
plotlines.obstacle: Rust's methods, personal moral compromises, family pressures
plotlines.stakes: career failure, loss of identity as good cop

plotlines.name: Marty: Family
plotlines.goal: maintain his marriage and family stability
plotlines.obstacle: his own infidelity, work obsession, inability to change
plotlines.stakes: divorce, losing his daughters, moral bankruptcy

plotlines.name: Errol: Conspiracy
plotlines.goal: continue the ritualistic murders and protect the cult
plotlines.obstacle: police investigation closing in, exposure of the network
plotlines.stakes: capture, end of the Yellow King tradition

events.event: Rust and Marty discover Dora Lange's ritualistic murder scene with occult symbols
Detectives examine the elaborate staging: prayer position, antlers, blue spiral, twig lattices
Investigation reveals Dora told Charlie Lange she would 'become a nun' and 'met a king'
Marie Fontenot case surfaces - girl disappeared five years ago with similar devil nets found
Child's report of 'green-eared spaghetti monster' chasing him through woods emerges
Rust expresses his nihilistic philosophy about human consciousness being a tragic misstep
Marty invites Rust to dinner, unaware it's his dead daughter's birthday
Reverend Tuttle's political connections raise suspicion as task force forms around anti-Christian elements
Black minister reports police ignored vandalism and animal mutilation at his church
2012 interview reveals Hart and Cohle haven't spoken in ten years after 2002 falling-out
Papania and Gilbough show photo of new victim posed similarly to Lange, questioning if real killer was caught
Present-day Rust appears weathered and alcoholic while Marty runs successful security firm

episodes.theme: the weight of truth and time

interactions.description: all storylines explore how confronting dark truths changes people over time
the audience sees connections between Tuttle and the occult elements that the detectives don't yet recognize
dual timeline structure reframes the 1995 investigation through the lens of its consequences

events.event: Cohle and Hart inform Dora's mother of her death, learning Dora claimed to attend church
They interview Carla who reveals Dora used drugs and mentioned a shelter near Spanish Lake
Major Quesada threatens to replace them with an occult-crimes task force
Rust insults the task force members and is reprimanded
Marty pleads for more time and secures a two-week deadline
They discover The Ranch brothel and obtain Dora's diary with references to King in Yellow and Carcosa
They find the Friends of Christ Revival flyer in the diary
Cohle deduces Hart is cheating with Lisa Tragnetti, creating friction between partners
They discover the abandoned church with the antler-crowned female mural matching Dora's pose
Marty's in-laws visit while Maggie expresses frustration about his absences
Marty's daughters play with dolls in a disturbing scene suggesting trauma awareness
Cohle reveals his daughter Sofia's death and marriage collapse to the 2012 investigators
Cohle explains his four years undercover and hospitalization after shooting cartel members
Cohle describes his ongoing chemical flashbacks from undercover work

interactions.description: all storylines explore how darkness corrupts innocence - the occult murders, Marty's daughters' disturbing play, and Rust's lost daughter
the case begins affecting Marty's family as his daughters show signs of trauma awareness
Rust knows about Marty's affair while Maggie remains unaware

events.event: Hart and Cohle locate preacher Joel Theriot and learn Dora Lange was seen with a tall scarred man
Detectives face pressure to turn the case over to the task force
Cohle searches old case files and discovers Rianne Olivier's suspicious death
Olivier's grandfather reveals she attended Light of the Way school owned by Reverend Tuttle
They learn Rianne ran off with Reggie Ledoux, a drug cook and sex offender connected to Charlie Lange
Hart begins reconnecting with Maggie despite her growing fascination with Cohle
Hart assaults Lisa's new boyfriend out of jealousy
Reggie Ledoux walks through the bayou in gas mask and underwear carrying a machete

episodes.theme: the gap between moral facade and true nature

interactions.description: all storylines explore hidden corruption beneath respectable surfaces - the cult behind the church, Hart's violence behind his family man image, the conspiracy behind official institutions
the audience sees Ledoux's true nature while the detectives are still piecing together his connection to the murders
the investigation leads directly to the conspiracy's operative, Ledoux, setting up their confrontation

events.event: Rust and Marty interrogate Charlie Lange about Reggie Ledoux
Charlie reveals Reggie discussed rich men's devil-worship involving sacrificing women and children
Charlie mentions 'Carcosa' and 'the Yellow King' and describes the spiral brand
Charlie admits showing Reggie explicit Polaroids of Dora
Marty tracks Tyrone Weems to a warehouse rave and violently confronts him
Tyrone reveals Reggie cooks exclusively for the Iron Crusaders biker gang
Cohle devises an off-the-books plan using his biker gang contacts
Cohle steals cocaine from the police evidence room to use as bait
Cohle reconnects with Ginger, posing as a former cartel security man
Ginger agrees to connect Cohle with Ledoux's supplier in exchange for helping rob a stash house
Cohle enters the stash house with bikers disguised as cops and steals drugs
A shootout erupts and the neighborhood descends into chaos
Cohle kidnaps Ginger and fights through houses to escape
Cohle leaps into Hart's getaway car at the last moment
Marty's affair with Lisa is exposed to Maggie
Marty's marriage unravels as he displays aggressive behavior toward both women
Marty refuses accountability and blames his father's death and alcohol
Papania and Gilbough question why Cohle took two weeks' leave at a critical moment
Both Cohle and Hart claim Cohle visited his sick father, but detectives note no hospital records

interactions.description: all storylines explore what characters sacrifice to achieve their goals - Rust risks his life and sanity for truth, Marty destroys his family through lies and violence
the audience knows the conspiracy exists through Charlie's revelations, but the detectives don't yet understand its full scope
Rust's dangerous undercover operation forces Marty to become his getaway driver, deepening their interdependence

events.event: Cohle and Ginger meet with DeWall Ledoux to discuss a drug deal
DeWall senses something dark in Cohle and refuses the deal
Hart follows DeWall to the remote compound in the bayou
Cohle and Hart storm the compound, avoiding explosive booby traps
Hart apprehends Reggie Ledoux and handcuffs him
Hart discovers two kidnapped children in horrific conditions
Hart executes Ledoux with a point-blank shot to the head
Ledoux's last words reference black stars and Carcosa
DeWall flees and is killed by his own land mine
Cohle fires assault rifle to create false shootout narrative
Both detectives are commended as heroes for rescuing the child
Hart has reconciled with Maggie and been promoted seven years later
Cohle has a girlfriend Laurie and is a renowned interrogator
Audrey acts out with dark clothing, smoking, and sexual activity
Marty slaps Audrey when she curses at him
Guy Francis confesses but claims Ledoux was not the real killer
Francis mentions the Yellow King connected to powerful people
Francis is found dead of apparent suicide after suspicious phone call
Cohle identifies officer Childress connected to Marie Fontenot case
Cohle discovers Yellow King symbols at abandoned Light of the Way school
Papania and Gilbough suspect Cohle has been behind the killings
They reveal Cohle was photographed at Lake Charles crime scene
They mention Cohle as person of interest in Tuttle's death
Cohle refuses access to his storage unit without a warrant

episodes.theme: the corruption of justice and the price of truth

interactions.description: all storylines explore how violence and moral compromise corrupt relationships and institutions
the audience knows the conspiracy is real while the 2012 investigators suspect Cohle
the Childress connection and Yellow King symbols link the past investigation to the ongoing conspiracy
dual timeline structure reveals how the 1995 'resolution' was actually incomplete

events.event: Cohle investigates missing persons cases and discovers connections to Tuttle's Wellspring Foundation
Cohle tracks down former preacher Theriot, who reveals Tuttle had photographs of nude children
Theriot explains he was intimidated into silence after reporting to Deacon Farrar in 1995
Cohle visits Kelly Reider at the institution and she describes a giant with scars
Kelly screams when Cohle asks about the scarred man's face
Cohle coldly tells an infanticide suspect to kill herself during interrogation
Marty refuses to engage with Cohle's harsh interrogation tactics
Marty confronts and beats two young men found with his underage daughter Audrey
Marty starts an affair with Beth, a former Ranch worker
Maggie discovers evidence of Marty's infidelity on his phone and in the laundry
Maggie attempts to sleep with a stranger at a bar but fails
Maggie goes to Rust's apartment and seduces him while he is vulnerable
Maggie tells Marty about her encounter with Rust
Marty attacks Rust in the police station parking lot, beating him badly
Rust abruptly quits the police force
In 2012, Marty leaves the station after refusing to believe claims about Rust's involvement
Maggie is interviewed in 2012 and defends Rust's character
Rust's truck pulls up behind Marty's car in 2012 and they exchange terse greetings
Rust suggests getting a drink with Marty

episodes.theme: the destructive power of obsession and betrayal

interactions.description: all storylines explore how obsessive pursuit of truth destroys relationships and moral boundaries
Maggie's seduction of Rust directly destroys the partnership between Rust and Marty
the audience knows Kelly's description of the scarred giant connects to Errol, but Rust doesn't yet make this connection

events.event: Rust reveals he's been 'functional but hammered' for ten years and returned because 'a man remembers his debts'
Rust shows Marty his elaborate conspiracy board with photographs, documents, and lattice structures
Rust theorizes the Dora Lange case connects to the Tuttle family and Shepherd's Flock preschool network
Rust reveals he conducted illegal break-ins at Reverend Tuttle's properties in 2010
Rust shows Marty the VHS tape of young Marie Fontenot being led to an altar in a ritualistic ceremony
Marty visits Maggie and thanks her, hinting he may not return from the dangerous investigation
Rust and Marty discover Marie Fontenot's missing persons file was mysteriously removed
They learn Deputy Steve Geraci mishandled Marie's 1995 report and Sheriff Childress may have suppressed the case
Miss Delores reveals the scarred man is likely a Tuttle family member named Childress or LeBeau
Delores cryptically references 'Carcosa' and 'the Yellow King' when shown lattice drawings
Papania and Gilbough unknowingly encounter Errol mowing grass in a spiral pattern near the abandoned church
Errol tells the detectives his family 'has been here a long, long time' while they fail to recognize him
Rust confesses he intends to resolve the case before ending his life, describing existence as meaningless
Rust and Marty confront corrupt Deputy Geraci at gunpoint on a boat

episodes.theme: the weight of unfinished business and moral debts

interactions.description: all storylines deal with cycles that must be completed — Rust's debt to the truth, Errol's generational ritual, the conspiracy's perpetual cover-up
the audience sees Errol hiding in plain sight while the investigating detectives remain oblivious
Rust's personal quest for meaning merges completely with solving the case — they become the same goal

events.event: Errol tortures his bound father while speaking in a British accent at his bayou compound
Rust and Marty force Sheriff Geraci to watch the Marie Fontenot VHS tape
Rust arranges for a sniper associate to monitor Geraci as insurance
Rust and Marty identify the green-eared spaghetti monster as a house painter with facial scars
They trace work orders to Childress and Son Maintenance, finding Errol's address
Marty discovers Billy Childress restrained with sewn lips in the compound house
Marty finds mummified remains, child clothing, and stick sculptures in Carcosa
Errol flees into the labyrinthine Carcosa structure
Rust pursues Errol into Carcosa and experiences a hallucinatory vortex vision
Errol ambushes and stabs Rust with a knife
Errol attacks Marty with a hatchet
Rust shoots Errol fatally despite severe wounds
Marty also shoots Errol
Forensic teams link Errol to dozens of missing persons cases
State attorney general discredits connections to Senator Tuttle's family
Marty's daughters visit him in the hospital
Rust describes his near-death experience feeling his daughter and father's love
Rust tells Marty that the light is winning, showing newfound hope

interactions.description: all storylines explore the battle between light and darkness — uncovering truth vs. hiding it, hope vs. despair, good vs. evil
the investigation storyline physically converges with the conspiracy at Carcosa for the final confrontation
Rust's near-death experience during the investigation becomes his spiritual transformation
